game_name: Tiny Toon Adventures 2: Trouble in Wackyland

element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Basic directional control and simple obstacle avoidance
Timed jumping between platforms and moving obstacles
High-speed traversal with limited control windows
Dodging enemy (Sweetie) on moving platform with precision timing
Consolidated platforming skills; multiple obstacle types; boss encounter with Montana Max
Resource management to unlock Funhouse final stage
Adaptive enemy patterns increase across stages

rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Escalating challenge across five distinct ride experiences with progressive complexity; final Funhouse stage consolidates all learned mechanics before Montana Max confrontation. Ticket-gated progression creates meta-rhythm of skill-building into resource accumulation.

difficulty_curve.early_game: Bumper car (Plucky) introduces foundational mechanics with gentler pacing; basic obstacle avoidance
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Train (Hamton) and Rollercoaster (Babs) introduce timed platforming and speed-based challenges; log ride (Furrball) adds persistent enemy threat requiring sustained dodging
difficulty_curve.late_game: Funhouse (Buster) consolidates all prior mechanics across multiple obstacle types and concludes with Montana Max boss fight; described by reviewers as 'deceptively challenging' and 'unforgiving'
notable_spikes: Transition from bumper car to train (introduces timed platforming)
Log ride enemy persistence (Sweetie constantly present)
Funhouse final stage complexity and boss encounter

notable_level_patterns.description: Each of five main stages features a unique Tiny Toons character piloting a different amusement park ride. Ride type determines movement constraints and obstacle patterns. Character abilities implied but not detailed in sources.
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Hamton's train ride with platform jumping

notable_level_patterns.description: Villainous characters (Roderick Rat, others) and environmental hazards increase in complexity across stages, culminating in Funhouse multi-hazard gauntlet
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Furrball's log ride with constant Sweetie enemy presence

notable_level_patterns.description: Player must accumulate tickets from ride performances to afford Funhouse entry. This creates a meta-progression rhythm separate from individual stage difficulty, forcing skill demonstration before accessing final content.
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Meta-stage between ride completions and Funhouse unlock

notable_level_patterns.description: Montana Max encounter in Funhouse after navigating consolidated platforming gauntlet. Reveals game's narrative frame (Montana Max as 'secret admirer' invitation sender).

sources_used: Wikipedia: Tiny Toon Adventures 2 - Gameplay section describing five ride stages and character roster
The Cutting Room Floor: Technical documentation confirming stage structure and hidden level select
